text:
Once there was a girl named Lily who loved writing. Every afternoon after school she would gather her notebook, a pen, and wander through her neighborhood, finding inspiration in the small things. One afternoon she stumbled upon a lonely tree house perched high above a serene pond at the edge of town. The tree house belonged to Old Man Jenkins, a recluse known only for his extensive collection of bird feathers.

Curiosity piqued, Lily decided to visit Old Man Jenkins. As she climbed into his cozy treehouse she listened to the peaceful water ripple against the gravel bed. Old Man Jenkins appeared momentarily, clutching a featherbed full of obsidian-black plumage.

"Isn't that beautiful?" Lily asked.

Old Man Jenkins looked at her solemnly. "The feathers tell many stories. Some birds flounce through life like autumn leaves. Others just sit and wait for spring. And some… well, some never sing at all."

Lily listened as he spun tales of birds she had never
